case_name: Estate of Gene B. Lokken et al. v. UnitedHealth Group / NaviHealth
court: United States District Court, District of Minnesota
status_at_anchor: Motion to dismiss granted in part Feb 13, 2025; discovery ongoing; scheduling order issued May 4, 2026

input.title: Lokken v. UnitedHealth — nH Predict algorithm Medicare Advantage claim denials
input.description: Plaintiffs allege UnitedHealth and its subsidiary NaviHealth used the 'nH Predict' AI algorithm to systematically deny medically-necessary post-acute care to Medicare Advantage members. Internal documents allegedly show employees instructed to keep determinations within 1% of algorithm predictions. Plaintiffs' deceased family members allegedly suffered or died after being forced out of skilled nursing facilities per algo recommendation contrary to physician judgment.

agents.name: UHC Claim Review Staff (1% deviation policy)

events.description: Algorithm generates length-of-stay prediction for post-acute care; trained on historical actuarial data, not physician-reviewed clinical outcomes
Internal policy allegedly requires UHC reviewers to keep determinations within 1% of nH Predict output regardless of clinical judgment
Reviewers fail to override algo prediction despite contrary physician documentation; institutional pressure cited
Coverage termination notices issued to plaintiffs while still medically indicated; appeals denied
Treating physicians' recommendations for continued care not honored; algo-driven denial upheld
